scope: LP-07 stream interruption and resume: dropped provider streams are classified as retryable, the sampling request is re-entered with unchanged prompt input, failed-attempt streamed tool state is abandoned with paired terminal events, retry attempts emit transcript-visible stream_error notices, and stale stream errors are cleared after a successful retry.

requiredBehaviors: A dropped stream whose cause is transient networking remains retryable after the provider stream has already emitted partial text.
The retry path re-enters the sampling request instead of retrying the raw SSE reader, preserving provider adapters' single-attempt stream semantics.
The re-entered sampling request receives the same prompt input when the failed partial stream did not commit durable assistant history.
edgeCases: ECONNRESET, socket-hang-up, fetch/network read failures, nested transient causes, and status/statusCode 5xx causes wrapped in StreamModelError classify as retryable.
Partial assistant_message_delta output from the failed attempt does not become durable assistant history.
Successful retry completion still emits turn_complete with the final assistant message from the successful attempt.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/session/run-turn.test.ts -t LP-07

requiredBehaviors: When a stream drops after emitting explicitly replay-safe streamed tool calls, the retry path aborts or discards the failed attempt's streaming tool executor before re-entering sampling.
Every tool_call_started event from a replay-safe failed attempt receives a paired tool_call_completed error event so observers do not retain orphaned in-flight tool calls.
Failed-attempt replay-safe tool blocks and tool results are cleared from the mutable turn state so a successful retry with no tools, or different tools, cannot execute stale work.

When streamed tool work is not explicitly read-only/replay-safe, the reconnect path refuses to resample, cancels queued tool work without dispatching it, and lets already-started work drain instead of duplicating side effects.
The live turn executor used by runTurn implements abort, discard, and queued-cancellation semantics; tests must not rely only on the standalone executor.
Drained tool results from a failed incomplete stream are surfaced as events only and are not persisted as orphan provider-visible tool messages.
Read-only tools that require user interaction are not treated as replay-safe.
edgeCases: A replay-safe failed attempt's synthetic tool result is not appended to durable model history because the partial stream was never committed.
In-flight replay-safe failed-attempt tools see an abort before they can finish stale work.
Retry completion with plain assistant text is allowed to finish without draining the abandoned executor.
A queued default side-effecting streamed tool is surfaced as a terminal error without being executed after the network drop.
A default side-effecting streamed tool that starts before the network drop is surfaced once and does not trigger a second sampling request.
A failed stream with already-started tool work leaves no standalone tool result in persisted session history.
An interactive read-only streamed tool does not reconnect and replay after a network drop.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/session/run-turn.test.ts -t LP-07

requiredBehaviors: Every accepted transient stream reconnect reservation emits a stream_error event before sleeping for backoff.
The stream_error payload identifies cause stream_disconnected, the active provider, and any HTTP status available on the underlying error.
Recovery re-entry reservation still gates the retry so the reconnect path respects the per-turn recovery cap.
edgeCases: A retry that is refused by the recovery re-entry cap emits no reconnect stream_error and lets the retry loop exhaust.
Errors with statusCode rather than status still expose the numeric stream_error status.
Errors without a status still emit a useful stream_error message and provider label.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/session/run-turn.test.ts -t LP-07

requiredBehaviors: A successful retry clears the prior attempt's lastStreamError before post-sample recovery runs.
The stale transport error cannot trigger streaming fallback or model fallback after the retry has already produced a valid assistant response.
Subsequent recovery decisions are based on the successful response's actual state rather than the failed attempt's transport cause.
edgeCases: Successful retries with normal assistant text clear lastStreamError.
Failed retries keep lastStreamError populated so the retry classifier and recovery ladder can still inspect the real cause.
Token-budget continuation decisions from a successful retry are preserved while only the stale transport error is cleared.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/session/run-turn.test.ts -t LP-07
